name: chat-bubble
description: Message bubble for chat and messaging UIs with avatar, name, and timestamp support

meta.name: ChatBubble
meta.description: Message bubble for chat and messaging UIs with avatar, name, and timestamp support

props.name: side
props.description: Edge the component is anchored to.

props.name: avatar
props.description: Avatar element shown beside the message.

props.name: name
props.description: Display name of the message sender.

props.name: time
props.description: Timestamp text shown with the message.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Incoming message
examples.code: <ChatBubble side="start" name="Alice" time="10:42 AM">Hey, how are you?</ChatBubble>
examples.description: Message from another user, aligned to the start

examples.title: Outgoing message
examples.description: Current user message, aligned to the end with accent background

examples.title: With avatar
examples.code: <ChatBubble side="start" avatar={<Avatar src="/alice.png" size="sm" />} name="Alice">See you tomorrow!</ChatBubble>
examples.description: Message with an avatar beside the bubble

whenToUse: Chat UIs and messaging features showing conversation history
Comment threads with a bubble-style layout
Customer support widgets displaying agent and user messages
whenNotToUse: General content cards without a conversational context — use Card
Inline comments that do not need a bubble visual treatment

antiPatterns.bad: Using ChatBubble for notification cards or feed items
antiPatterns.why: ChatBubble implies a back-and-forth conversation; use Card for standalone content pieces

related.name: Card
related.reason: Use for general content display that is not part of a conversation

related.name: Avatar
related.reason: Pass an Avatar as the avatar prop to identify the message sender

intent.a11yRationale: Pure layout component with role="none". Wrap a list of ChatBubble elements in an element with role="log" and aria-live="polite" to announce new messages to screen readers.

flexibility.note: Accepts any ReactNode — Avatar, initials, icon, or nothing
children accepts rich content including images, links, and formatted text
